title: Deprecate `--columns` flag in `update cells` in favor of rest arguments
body:
## Summary

- Add rest arguments to `update cells` for specifying columns to update, matching the pattern used by `into` commands and other filters
- Deprecate the `--columns` (`-c`) flag via `deprecation_info()`, following the same pattern as `select`, `reject`, and `get`
- The old `--columns` flag still works for backward compatibility but emits a deprecation warning

### Before

```nushell
[[name val1 val2]; [foo 1 4] [bar 8 3]] | update cells -c [val1 val2] { $in * 2 }
```

### After

```nushell
[[name val1 val2]; [foo 1 4] [bar 8 3]] | update cells { $in * 2 } val1 val2
```

### New examples

```nushell
# Update specific columns in a record
{a: 1, b: 2, c: 3} | update cells { $in + 10 } a c
# => {a: 11, b: 2, c: 13}
```

## Release notes summary

`update cells` now accepts column names as rest arguments instead of requiring the `--columns` flag. The `--columns` flag is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Closes #15477
